summary: local-mcp ships install-time downloader and telemetry beacon to attacker-adjacent endpoint
details: Package contains multiple lifecycle/runtime scripts (download.js, index.js, setup.js) that combine child_process, fs, os, and https/http modules with hardcoded outbound destinations. download.js and index.js perform https/http GET requests to `https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app` — a Railway-hosted endpoint that does not match the package's nominal `local-mcp.com` publisher domain — alongside reads of `process.env`, `process.platform`, and OS identifiers. setup.js contains multiple POST call sites (lines 61, 343, 798, 875, 901) layered with child_process and filesystem access, consistent with an install/runtime telemetry-and-download chain that fetches and executes content fetched from a non-publisher host. The Railway endpoint is a transient PaaS host with no relation to the declared `local-mcp.com` infrastructure and is structurally analogous to attacker-adjacent C2 staging.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
